expectations.text: CollectionView displays messages from an ObservableCollection
expectations.evidence: ChatViewModel.cs line 15 declares `public ObservableCollection<Message> Messages { get; } = new();`. ChatPage.xaml line 85 binds `ItemsSource="{Binding Messages}"` on the CollectionView named `MessagesCollectionView`. This is a genuine binding to a live ObservableCollection, not a stub.

expectations.text: Message alignment differs by sender — right-aligned for current user, left-aligned for others
expectations.evidence: MessageTemplateSelector.cs returns `MyMessageTemplate` when `IsFromMe == true` and `TheirMessageTemplate` otherwise. In ChatPage.xaml: MyMessageTemplate places the Border in `Grid.Column="1"` with a `Width="*"` spacer column on the left (lines 18-38), pushing content right. TheirMessageTemplate places the Border in `Grid.Column="0"` with the spacer on the right (lines 42-70), pushing content left. The selector is wired via `ItemTemplate="{StaticResource MessageTemplateSelector}"`.

expectations.text: Code calls CollectionView.ScrollTo() to scroll to the last message when new messages arrive
expectations.evidence: ChatPage.xaml.cs lines 30-33: `MessagesCollectionView.ScrollTo(item: message, position: ScrollToPosition.End, animate: true);` is called in the `ScrollToBottom(Message message)` method. This method is invoked as a callback registered with the ViewModel, which fires it whenever the CollectionChanged event fires for new items.

expectations.text: A CollectionChanged or property-change handler triggers the scroll behavior
expectations.evidence: ChatViewModel.cs line 39: `Messages.CollectionChanged += OnMessagesCollectionChanged;`. The handler `OnMessagesCollectionChanged` (lines 74-85) checks for `NotifyCollectionChangedAction.Add`, extracts the new message, and calls `_scrollToBottomCallback?.Invoke(newMessage)` on the main thread. This directly ties every new item added to the scroll action.

expectations.text: Message model has properties for content/text, a sender or IsFromMe flag, and optionally timestamp
expectations.evidence: Message.cs defines: `public string Text { get; set; }` (content), `public bool IsFromMe { get; set; }` (sender/direction flag), `public string SenderName { get; set; }` (sender identity), and `public DateTime Timestamp { get; set; }` (optional timestamp). All required properties are present.

claims.claim: DataTemplateSelector is used to route left/right bubble templates
claims.evidence: MessageTemplateSelector.cs implements DataTemplateSelector, and ChatPage.xaml wires it as `ItemTemplate="{StaticResource MessageTemplateSelector}"` with both MyMessageTemplate and TheirMessageTemplate properties populated.

claims.claim: ScrollTo is called on the UI thread via MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread
ViewModel has no direct UI dependency — scroll is triggered via a callback delegate
claims.evidence: ChatViewModel.cs uses `Action<Message>? _scrollToBottomCallback` (line 13) registered via `SetScrollToBottomCallback`. The ViewModel never references any UI type. ChatPage.xaml.cs (lines 18, 25-34) owns the actual `MessagesCollectionView.ScrollTo(...)` call.

claims.claim: x:DataType is applied to both DataTemplate resources for compiled bindings
claims.evidence: ChatPage.xaml line 16: `x:DataType="models:Message"` on MyMessageTemplate. Line 43: `x:DataType="models:Message"` on TheirMessageTemplate.

claims.claim: EmptyView is wrapped in ContentView per skill gotcha guidance
No ViewCell is used in any template
claims.evidence: Inspected all DataTemplate content in ChatPage.xaml — templates use Grid, Border, VerticalStackLayout, Label. No ViewCell element anywhere in the file.

suggestions.assertion: Message alignment differs by sender — right-aligned for current user, left-aligned for others
suggestions.reason: The assertion passes based on XAML column placement, but no assertion checks that the DataTemplateSelector actually routes correctly at runtime (i.e., that IsFromMe=true selects MyMessageTemplate). The selector logic is simple and correct here, but a stronger assertion would specify that the template selector returns the correct template based on the IsFromMe flag, not just that two templates with different column placement exist.

No assertion verifies that the scroll actually targets the *last* message (i.e., the most recently added item). The code correctly uses `e.NewItems[e.NewItems.Count - 1]` to get the last added item, but the expectation only says 'scrolls to the last message when new messages arrive' without checking this detail. A transcript-only check could pass even if the code always scrolled to the first item.

No assertion covers thread safety — the skill explicitly flags that ScrollTo must be called on the UI thread. The code correctly uses MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread, which is a meaningful correctness property. This is worth asserting since omitting it is a common bug.

eval_feedback.overall: All five assertions pass with strong evidence. The evals cover the core functional requirements well. The main gap is that alignment and scroll-target correctness rely on structural inspection rather than behavioral verification. Adding assertions about DataTemplateSelector routing logic and UI-thread safety for scroll would make the eval more discriminating.
